description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Simplifies the remote worker connect flow so workspace setup feels clearer and lighter.
- Moves local workspace ownership into OpenWork server so worker state and onboarding stay more reliable across app surfaces.
Fixes & polish
- Polishes session tool traces by moving the chevron affordance to the right for easier scanning.
- Adds Windows arm64 pnpm dev support for desktop contributors.
- Refreshes README and architecture docs to match the current workspace model.
Packaging / ops
- Publishes the v0.11.182 package set across app, desktop, server, orchestrator, and opencode-router.
- Carries forward the matching AUR packaging update for this release line.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.181...v0.11.182
